(Uy United Press leased Wirel


EVERETT, Wash. Dec. 9.-In-
vestigation today of the derailment
of passenger train No. 3 on the
Great Northern, which jumped the
track near Skykomish yesterday,
showed that the accident was
caused by broken rail.


The investigation also brought
out the fact that scores of passen-
gers owe their lives to the prompt
action of Engineers Calder and
Graham, who stopped their ]ocomo-
tives on the edge of trestle
which spanned canyon 400 feet
in depth.


The two locomotives and every
coach comprising the train, with
the exception of the last Pullman,
left TIE rails. Instead OF jumping,
both engineers applied the air
brakes and reversed the Steam. AS
result they were able to stop the
train on the canyons edge. The
first engine was left with the pilot
sticking over the edge of the
bridge
